definitions.text: To hide, withdraw, or be concealed.
to depart secretly and hide oneself
After performing an epic SAFETY ROLL out of danger, I shouted to Bruno 'Nice abscond bro'
To give pain or sorrow to; afflict; grieve.
To bear hard upon; oppress or injure in one's rights; vex or harass, as by injustice: used chiefly or only in the passive.
To provoke, or cause grieving upon someone. Usually not physically, but in some other way. Like verbally, or politically even.
One of Moskovicks' most employed didactic methods is aggrieving her students perpetually for any reason she can find
People who care about Climate Change were aggrieved when they found out that Trump revoked the EPA finding on greenhouse gasses
To hinder; to withhold.
'Hahhaaha-', no I must not lauph yet, I must abstain from my lauphter.
To commit the first act of hostility or offense; to begin a quarrel or controversy; to make an attack
take the initiative and go on the offensive, as in a game
I pushed my luck, deciding to agress Lucio by raising the stakes in our game of poker
to terminate a procedure prematurely
given the unfortunate defects it had, the mother decided to abort the cake-baking process and start all over again
To renounce upon oath or an idea
The political divide i've seen across this earth has led me to abjure both the left and the right parties.
To renounce upon a position, status or responsability
Fede simply couldn't take it, he had way too many irons in the fire, he was forced to abdicate his possision as the CEO of the CIA
To feel strong or immoderate desire for somethign (could be something that is of another).
I personally covet Dani's outstanding conscientiousness personality trait
Servile flattery; excessive or unmerited praise; exaggerated compliment
Chiara was merely adulating him in hopes she would get a free drink in return
To formally call someone before a court to answer a criminal charge
Megan was arraigned to court, she had to chose weather to plead guilty, or not guilty
Dictated by greatness of mind or heart; exhibiting nobleness of soul; liberal and honorable; unselfish
Mili took a magnanimous approach to the whole ordeal, deciding to give my collegues cupkakes with meaningful messages on them.
False; untrue: synonym: dishonest.

difficult to detect or grasp by the mind or analyze
skillful at eluding capture
making great mental demands; hard to comprehend or solve or believe

free from defect, disease, or infirmity. Retaining exceptional health and vigor
But unbeknownst to you, i've been maintaining myself hale and hearty all along

caustic, sarcastic, or bitter

Liveliness; briskness; sprightliness.

generally incompetent and ineffectual

Generous; kind

Look at this whole as conflagration you just caused at our house bro

A playful, lighthearted jump or leap(Can also be used as a verb in that regard); a frivolous or mischievous act or escapade. It can also refer to a criminal scheme or heist, particularly in informal usage. Additionally, in culinary contexts, a caper is a small pickled flower bud used as a condiment or seasoning in cooking.
The children capered around the playground, shrieking with laughter and chasing each other through the sprinklers.
The elaborate bank caper was meticulously planned over months, yet fell apart within minutes of execution.
The chef finished the dish with a handful of capers, adding a sharp, briny contrast to the rich creamy sauce.
